era: 1996-present. Radio host/DJ. E=4 -- adventurous, kinetic, border-crossing energy. Wo' Pop. KEXP's world music specialist. Africa, South America, Middle East, Asia. Expanding KEXP beyond indie rock.

location.formative: KEXP 90.3 FM (formerly KCMU). Capitol Hill. The new KEXP building at Seattle Center (opened 2016). Wo' Pop studio.
location.career: KEXP Wo' Pop host (1996-present). World music specialist. Global music curation. Live in-studio sessions with international artists.

members.name: Darek Mazzone
members.note: KEXP's world music specialist since 1996. Wo' Pop explores global music: Africa, South America, Middle East, Asia, Caribbean, Eastern Europe. The geographic expander. Completes the KEXP host trio (130-132).

associated_musicians.name: Cheryl Waters
associated_musicians.note: The Midday Show. Local/indie focus. KEXP host trio: Waters (130), Richards (131), Mazzone (132). Waters is the local anchor; Mazzone is the global frontier.

associated_musicians.name: John Richards
associated_musicians.note: The Morning Show. Personal, vulnerable. The emotional center of the trio. Mazzone is the geographic expander.

associated_musicians.name: Bruce Pavitt
associated_musicians.note: Sub Pop co-founder. E=3 curation (indie/alternative). Mazzone applies the same curatorial instinct to global music at E=4.

associated_musicians.name: Nasty Nes Rodriguez
associated_musicians.note: KCMU hip-hop DJ, E=4. Nasty Nes expanded KCMU into hip-hop. Mazzone expands KEXP into world music. Same station, same mission, same energy.

associated_musicians.name: Pat O'Day
associated_musicians.note: KJR AM, E=4. O'Day and Mazzone share E=4 energy but opposite approaches: mainstream commercial entertainment vs. independent global curation.

theory_nodes.concept: KEXP host trio completes (130-132) -- three consecutive on-air hosts documenting the station's personality infrastructure: Waters (130, Midday, local), Richards (131, Morning, personal), Mazzone (132, Wo' Pop, global); local + personal + global = KEXP's full mission
examples: Waters (130): The Midday Show. Seattle-focused. Indie/alternative core. E=3.
Richards (131): The Morning Show. Personal, vulnerable. Music Heals. E=3.
Mazzone (132): Wo' Pop. Global music. Border-crossing. E=4.
Together: local + personal + global = KEXP's full mission. Trio complete.
theory_nodes.description: KEXP host trio completes at degree 132. Three facets of one curatorial mission.

ancestors: KCMU/KEXP institutional history (126-128). Station as cultural infrastructure.
Waters (130) opened the trio. Richards (131) added the personal dimension.
genealogy.relationship: Host trio completes at degree 132. KEXP on-air personality infrastructure fully documented.

theory_nodes.concept: Global music curation -- Wo' Pop as geographic expander; not 'world music' as homogeneous category but regional traditions with specificity; Africa, South America, Middle East, Asia, Caribbean, Eastern Europe; cross-pollination and fusion at the intersections
examples: West Africa: highlife, Afrobeat, Mande, desert blues. Deepest region in rotation.
South America: cumbia, tropicalia, MPB, chicha. Rhythmic richness.
Middle East/N. Africa: gnawa, rai, Turkish psych. Adventurous territory.
Cross-pollination: Afro-electronic, global bass, diaspora music at intersections.
theory_nodes.description: Global music curation. Regional specificity. Geographic expansion of KEXP's identity.

ancestors: KEXP's curatorial tradition from KCMU freeform.
Nasty Nes (126) expanded KCMU into hip-hop -- same genre-expansion impulse.
genealogy.relationship: Global curation at degree 132. The geographic frontier of KEXP's mission.

theory_nodes.concept: The DJ as translator -- making the unfamiliar accessible without reducing it to exotic novelty; context, connection, and selection as translation tools; respecting the source while bridging the cultural gap; the strongest version of the human curator argument
examples: Malian kora recording with context about the griot tradition.
Cumbia track placed in the history of tropical dance music.
Selection of tracks compelling regardless of cultural familiarity.
KEXP's argument: if human curation can guide you through gnawa, it can guide you through anything.
theory_nodes.description: DJ as translator. Making unfamiliar music accessible with respect and context.

ancestors: Human curator vs. algorithm (131). Richards' vulnerability-based argument.
Wo' Pop is the strongest test case: the most unfamiliar music requires the most skilled curation.
genealogy.relationship: DJ as translator at degree 132. The ultimate test of the human curation argument.

theory_nodes.concept: E=4 adventurous DJ energy -- kinetic, exploratory, border-crossing; higher than KEXP's E=3 baseline; rhythmically driven world music demands higher host energy; DJ as travel guide through new terrain; not commercial E=5 but KEXP-calibrated E=4
examples: Afrobeat, cumbia, gnawa are more rhythmically energetic than indie/folk.
The host's energy matches the music -- more animated than Waters or Richards.
E=4 matches Nasty Nes (126) and Pat O'Day (129) at the on-air performer level.
Not commercial E=5 -- still listener-supported, contextual, respectful.
theory_nodes.description: E=4 adventurous DJ energy. Kinetic, border-crossing. Matches the music.

ancestors: E=3 calibrated at Waters (130) and Richards (131) for KEXP's quiet baseline.
E=4 at Nasty Nes (126) for on-air DJ energy. Mazzone at same level.
genealogy.relationship: E=4 DJ energy at degree 132. KEXP's highest on-air energy in the host trio.

cross_references.relationship: KEXP HOST TRIO! Waters (Midday, 130, local, E=3) and Mazzone (Wo' Pop, 132, global, E=4). The geographic poles of KEXP. Steady warmth vs. adventurous energy.
KEXP HOST TRIO COMPLETE! Richards (Morning, 131, personal, E=3) and Mazzone (Wo' Pop, 132, global, E=4). Emotional center vs. geographic frontier. Together with Waters: local + personal + global.

CURATORIAL LINEAGE! Pavitt (E=3, indie/alternative curation) and Mazzone (E=4, global music curation). Same instinct -- identifying what matters in the noise. Different musical territories, same curatorial DNA.
GENRE EXPANSION LINEAGE! Nasty Nes (KCMU, 126, E=4, hip-hop) and Mazzone (KEXP, 132, E=4, world music). Both expanded the station's sonic boundaries at E=4. Same station, same mission, same energy.
ENERGY COMPARISON! O'Day (KJR AM, 129, E=4, mainstream) and Mazzone (KEXP FM, 132, E=4, global). Same energy, opposite approaches. Commercial entertainment vs. independent global curation.
